content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Isabel Sewell 
Friends of Library 
800 Alma st. 
Menlo Park, CA 
94025 
Dear Ms. Sewell: 
October 3, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-544 
You have requested advice regarding the campaign provisions 
of the Political Reform Act of 1974. 1 
QUESTION 
Where does the Friends of Menlo Park Library file the Form 
461 as an independent expenditure committee? 
CONCLUSION 
If the Friends of Menlo Park qualifies as a city independent 
expenditure committee, it must file its reporting statements with 
the city clerk's office of the City of Menlo Park. 
FACTS 
After reviewing our advice letter No. 1-89-449, you have 
determined that the Friends of Menlo Park Library will qualify as 
an independent expenditure committee. 
As such, you intend to file 
the Form 461. 
ANALYSIS 
section 84215 states, in part, that all committees formed or 
existing primarily to support or oppose candidates or local 
measures to be voted upon in one city, and city general purpose 
committees, including independent expenditure committees, shall 
file the original and one copy of their campaign reports with the 
clerk of the city. 
1 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


Isabel Sewell 
Page Two 
If you have any additional questions, please call me at (916) 
322-5662. 
Sincerely, 
B 
Donov 
unsel 
~:aat~~~ 
cal Reform Consultant 




1 
lHF 
ISABEL SEWEll F P P C 
Ser 19 
I 23 11M '99 
I j
